channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Here's where Trump's approval rating stands on key issues
item.description: President Trump's approval ratings are showing signs of weakening nearly two months into his second term. While his efforts to crack down on immigration seem largely well-received, Trump’s performance on the economy has taken a hit amid whipsawing tariff plans followed by stock market losses.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, Fox 5

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent passengers fleeing onto a wing in a fraught evacuation amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN, COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: 911 audio released in Idaho student murders
item.description: Authorities have unveiled the recording of a panicked 911 call placed after the stabbings of four University of Idaho students in November 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Justice Department is investigating whether Columbia University hid students sought by the US
item.description: A top U.S. Justice Department official says the department is investigating whether Columbia University concealed “illegal aliens” on its campus.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: John Feinstein, bestselling author and one of the country's foremost sports writers, dies at 69
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine, but he emphasized that the terms are yet to be worked out and added that any truce should pave the way to lasting peace.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, ALEXA ST. JOHN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search ends for Dane Paulsen after volunteer diver recovers body
item.description: Dane Paulsen, a 2-year-old boy, was found dead in an area near his family home in Siletz, Ore., after a massive search effort spanning weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors say Kohberger's defense will argue evidence was planted in Idaho student murders case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors say Bryan Kohberger’s defense team will argue at his murder trial that someone else could have planted a knife sheath with Kohberger’s DNA at the home where four University of Idaho students were killed in 2022.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, Fox 5

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
